title: Prospecting Biochemical Pathways to Implement Microbe-Based Production of the New-to-Nature Platform Chemical Levulinic Acid
authors: Vila-Santa, A, Islam, MA, Ferreira, FC, Prather, KLJ, Mira, NP
abstract: Exploring biochemical pathways for sustainable production of levulinic acid using microbial systems, with applications in green chemistry and renewable resources.

title: Genetic and metabolic engineering challenges of C1-gas fermenting acetogenic chassis organisms
authors: Bourgade, B, Minton, NP, Islam, MA
abstract: Comprehensive review of the challenges and opportunities in engineering acetogenic bacteria for gas fermentation and sustainable chemical production.

title: Synergistic substrate cofeeding stimulates reductive metabolism
authors: Park, JO, Liu, N, Holinski, KM, Emerson, DF, Qiao, K, Woolston, BM, Xu, J, Lazar, Z, Islam, MA, Vidoudez, C, Girguis, PR, Stephanopoulos, G
abstract: Demonstrating how combined substrate feeding strategies can enhance microbial metabolic efficiency and production yields in engineered systems.

title: Synthetic biology strategies for improving microbial synthesis of 'green' biopolymers
authors: Anderson, LA, Islam, MA, Prather, KLJ
abstract: Review of promising synthetic biology approaches for sustainable production of environmentally friendly biodegradable polymers.

title: Exploring biochemical pathways for mono-ethylene glycol (MEG) synthesis from synthesis gas
authors: Islam, MA, Hadadi, N, Ataman, M, Hatzimanikatis, V, Stephanopoulos, G
abstract: Systematic analysis of metabolic pathways and engineering strategies for microbial production of mono-ethylene glycol, an important industrial chemical.

title: New insights into Dehalococcoides mccartyi metabolism from a reconstructed metabolic network-based systems-level analysis of D. mccartyi transcriptomes
authors: Islam, MA, Waller, AS, Hug, LA, Provart, NJ, Edwards, EA, Mahadevan, R
abstract: Systems-level analysis providing new insights into the metabolic capabilities and regulatory mechanisms of Dehalococcoides mccartyi for bioremediation applications.
